### Secure Traffic Option in TypeScript

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.9.1/sandbox/page.mdx

Determines whether to secure all traffic coming to the sandbox controller with an authentication token. Defaults to false.

```typescript
optional secure: boolean;

// Default
false
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Default Secure Traffic Value

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.8.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Sets the default value for the 'secure' property to false, meaning traffic is not secured by default.

```typescript
false
```

--------------------------------

### Secure Traffic Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.2/sandbox/page.mdx

Enables or disables security for all traffic coming to the sandbox controller using an auth token. Defaults to false.

```typescript
optional secure: boolean;

// Default: false
```

--------------------------------

### E2B Sandbox Security Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Secures all traffic to the sandbox controller with an auth token. Defaults to false.

```typescript
optional secure: boolean;
```

--------------------------------

### TypeScript: Define Optional Secure Traffic Flag for Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.3/sandbox/page.mdx

Defines an optional 'secure' boolean property for E2B sandboxes. When set to true, it enforces security for all traffic to the sandbox controller using an authentication token. The default value is false.

```typescript
optional secure: boolean;
// Default: false

```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox Creation with Explicit Security Settings

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/migration/v2/page.mdx

Shows how to explicitly disable the default secure communication for sandboxes in both JavaScript and Python SDKs when direct URL access is required.

```javascript
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

const sandbox = await Sandbox.create({ secure: false }) // Explicitly disable
```

```python
from e2b_code_interpreter import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox.create(secure=False)  # Explicitly disable
```

--------------------------------

### Secure Traffic Configuration for E2B Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.10.0/sandbox/page.mdx

This snippet shows the optional boolean flag to secure all traffic to the sandbox controller with an auth token. The default value is `false`.

```typescript
optional secure: boolean;
```

```typescript
false
```

--------------------------------

### Enable Secure Traffic with Auth Token

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.13.0/sandbox/page.mdx

When enabled, this option secures all traffic coming to the sandbox controller with an authentication token. The default value is false.

```typescript
optional secure: boolean;

// Secure all traffic coming to the sandbox controller with auth token
// Default: false
```

--------------------------------

### Secure Traffic Option for E2B Sandbox Controller

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.5.1/sandbox/page.mdx

This code defines the `secure` option for an E2B sandbox controller. When set to `true`, it enables authentication using an auth token for all traffic coming to the sandbox controller. The default value is `false`.

```ts
optional secure: boolean;

// Default
false

```

--------------------------------

### Download Data from Sandbox using Pre-signed URLs

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/filesystem/download/page.mdx

This method generates a secure, time-limited URL to download files from the sandbox, allowing access from unauthorized environments like a browser. It requires creating a sandbox with `secure: true`. The input is the file path and an optional expiration time, and the output is a public URL to the file.

```javascript
import fs from 'fs'
import { Sandbox } from '@e2b/code-interpreter'

// Start a secured sandbox (all operations must be authorized by default)
const sandbox = await Sandbox.create(template, { secure: true })

// Create a pre-signed URL for file download with a 10 second expiration
const publicUrl = await sandbox.downloadUrl(
  'demo.txt', {
    useSignatureExpiration: 10_000, // optional
  },
)

// Download a file with a pre-signed URL (this can be used in any environment, such as a browser)
const res = await fetch(publicUrl)
const content = await res.text()
```

```python
from e2b import Sandbox

# Start a secured sandbox (all operations must be authorized by default)
sandbox = Sandbox.create(timeout=12_000, secure=True)

# Create a pre-signed URL for file download with a 10 second expiration
# The user only has to visit the URL to download the file, this also works in a browser.
signed_url = sbx.download_url(path="demo.txt", user="user", use_signature_expiration=10_000)
```

--------------------------------

### Configure API Keys for E2B and Claude

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/code-interpreting/analyze-data-with-ai/page.mdx

Sets up environment variables with E2B and Claude API keys. These keys are essential for authentication and accessing the respective services. Ensure these are stored securely in a .env file.

```bash
E2B_API_KEY=e2b_***
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-***
```

--------------------------------

### E2B Sandbox URL Options - Signature Usage Configuration

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.11.0/sandbox/page.mdx

Enables the use of a signature for the sandbox URL. This is required when using secured envd in a sandbox. Defaults to false.

```typescript
optional useSignature: true;
```

--------------------------------

### Signature Usage for Sandbox URL Generation

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/js-sdk/v1.10.0/sandbox/page.mdx

This snippet shows the optional configuration to use a signature for sandbox URLs. This is necessary when using secured envd in the sandbox. The default is `false`.

```typescript
optional useSignature: true;
```

```typescript
false
```

--------------------------------

### Create a new E2B Sandbox instance

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.0.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Instantiates a new secure and isolated E2B cloud sandbox. It allows customization of the template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, security settings, and internet access. Use this method instead of the constructor.

```python
from e2b import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox.create(
    template="<your-template-id>",
    timeout=600,
    metadata={"user_id": "123"},
    envs={"MY_VAR": "my_value"},
    secure=True,
    allow_internet_access=False
)
```

--------------------------------

### List Sandboxes using SandboxApi

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.5.4/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Retrieves a list of all running sandboxes. Supports filtering by metadata, domain specification, debug mode, request timeout, custom headers, and proxy configuration. It defaults to using the E2B_API_KEY environment variable for authentication.

```python
class SandboxApi(SandboxApiBase)

@classmethod
async def list(cls,
               api_key: Optional[str] = None,
               query: Optional[SandboxQuery] = None,
               domain: Optional[str] = None,
               debug: Optional[bool] = None,
               request_timeout: Optional[float] = None,
               headers: Optional[Dict[str, str]] = None,
               proxy: Optional[ProxyTypes] = None) -> List[ListedSandbox]:
    # ... implementation details ...
```

--------------------------------

### Sandbox API - List Sandboxes

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v1.1.0/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Lists all currently running sandboxes. Authentication can be provided via an API key or defaults to an environment variable.

```APIDOC
## GET /sandboxes

### Description
Lists all running sandboxes.

### Method
GET

### Endpoint
/sandboxes

### Parameters
#### Query Parameters
- **api_key** (string) - Optional - API key to use for authentication, defaults to `E2B_API_KEY` environment variable.
- **domain** (string) - Optional - The domain to list sandboxes from.
- **debug** (boolean) - Optional - Enable debug mode for listing sandboxes.
- **request_timeout** (float) - Optional - Timeout for the request in seconds.

### Request Example
```json
{
  "api_key": "YOUR_API_KEY",
  "domain": "example.com",
  "debug": true,
  "request_timeout": 30.0
}
```

### Response
#### Success Response (200)
- **sandboxes** (array[SandboxInfo]) - List of running sandboxes.

##### SandboxInfo
- **name** (string) - The name of the sandbox.
- **id** (string) - The unique identifier of the sandbox.

#### Response Example
```json
{
  "sandboxes": [
    {
      "name": "my-sandbox-1",
      "id": "sbx_123abc"
    },
    {
      "name": "another-sandbox",
      "id": "sbx_456def"
    }
  ]
}
```
```

--------------------------------

### Create Sandbox Instance - Python

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.0.2/sandbox_async/page.mdx

Creates a new sandbox instance with configurable options. Supports specifying a template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, security settings, and internet access. Defaults to a secure sandbox created from the default base template.

```python
await AsyncSandbox.create(
    template='template-name',
    timeout=300,
    metadata={'key': 'value'},
    envs={'VAR_NAME': 'value'},
    secure=True,
    allow_internet_access=True
)
```

--------------------------------

### Create a New E2B Sandbox

Source: https://github.com/e2b-dev/e2b/blob/main/apps/web/src/app/(docs)/docs/sdk-reference/python-sdk/v2.1.1/sandbox_sync/page.mdx

Creates a new E2B cloud sandbox. Sandboxes can be configured with a specific template, timeout, metadata, environment variables, security settings, and internet access. Defaults to the 'base' template with a 300-second timeout, secure access, and internet enabled.

```python
from e2b import Sandbox

sandbox = Sandbox.create(
    template="<your-template-name>",
    timeout=600,
    metadata={"user_id": "123"},
    envs={"MY_VAR": "value"},
    secure=True,
    allow_internet_access=True
)
```
